param.name: input_inundation_boundary
param.dialogReference: Raw inundation boundary that will be used to generate the evacuation boundaries

param.name: eliminate_polygon_area_size
param.displayname: Maximum polygon area to remove polygon parts
param.dialogReference:
Polygon parts (holes and standalone polygons) smaller than the specified area will be removed. Holes are filled and standalone polygons are deleted.

Further documentation can be found here: Eliminate Polygon Parts

param.name: eliminate_polygon_area_unit
param.displayname: Area unit for removing polygon parts
param.dialogReference: Defines the unit to be used to remove polygon parts.

param.name: eliminate_polygon_area_contained_parts
param.dialogReference: Specifies whether the polgyon parts that will be removed are contained or non-contained parts. Further documentation can be found here: Eliminate Polygon Parts

param.name: buffer_distance
param.dialogReference: This parameter specifies the buffer distance which is applied to the raw inundation boundary. A positive buffer is used to merge unconnected flood areas, then a negative buffer is applied to preserve the general shape of the flood extents.

param.name: buffer_unit
param.dialogReference: Defines the unit to be used for the buffer distance parameter.

param.name: smoothing_tolerance
param.dialogReference:
Specifies the smoothing tolerance, in feet, that will be applied to the evacuation boundary.

Further documentation can be found here: Smoothing Polygon

param.name: outer_buffer_distance
param.dialogReference: Specifies a positive buffer distance that will be applied to the evacuation boundary.

param.name: outer_buffer_unit
param.dialogReference: Defines the unit to be used for the outer region buffer.

param.name: output_gdb
param.dialogReference: Specifies where the outputs of the tool will be saved.
